title: GAD-7 Anxiety Self-Assessment
description: A brief validated measure to screen for generalized anxiety disorder. This 7-item questionnaire helps you understand your current anxiety levels.
instructions: Over the past 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems?

questions.text: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying
Worrying too much about different things
Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
Feeling afraid, as if something awful might happen

bands.label: Minimal Anxiety
bands.description: Your responses suggest minimal anxiety. Continue your current wellness practices and self-care routines.

bands.label: Mild Anxiety
bands.description: Your responses suggest mild anxiety. Self-help strategies like those in this course can be very effective. Monitor your symptoms over time.

bands.label: Moderate Anxiety
bands.description: Your responses suggest moderate anxiety. Consider combining the techniques in this course with professional support for the best outcomes.

bands.label: Severe Anxiety
bands.description: Your responses suggest severe anxiety. We strongly recommend seeking a professional evaluation alongside the educational content in this course.

scoring.functionalImpairmentQuestion: If you checked any problems, how difficult have they made it for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people?

disclaimer: This is an educational self-check tool, not a clinical diagnosis. Results are meant to help you reflect on your experiences. If you are in crisis or need immediate help, please call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
